title: Hispana (rapper)

infobox.value: is a Mexican rapper, singer and songwriter from [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), and is one of the most prominent female artists in the Mexican rap scene.[[1]](#cite_note-Vice-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Slang-2)[[3]](#cite_note-IndieRocks-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Vice2-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Chidas-5) Influenced by North American hip-hop but rooted in Spanish rap, Hispana's lyrics reflect and amplify everyday life and are imbued with a social and feminist conscience.[[6]](#cite_note-RollingStone2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-poua-7)
["Recomendaciones de diez estrellas latinas para despedir el 2020."](https://www.vice.com/es/article/recomendaciones-de-diez-estrellas-latinas-para-despedir-el-2020/).
["Hispana: la Mamba Negra que cambió de piel a Mujer de Fuego."](https://www.chidas.mx/2020/05/hispana-la-mamba-negra-que-cambio-de-piel-a-mujer-de-fuego/).
["Hispana nos cuenta de su próximo disco y lo que le atrajo del rap"](https://www.slang.fm/videos/hispana-nos-cuenta-de-su-proximo-disco-lo-que-le-atrajo-del-rap-y-como-eligio-su-nombre-slangcam/).

first_paragraph: **Hispana**, also known as **Mamba Negra** is a Mexican rapper, singer and songwriter from [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), and is one of the most prominent female artists in the Mexican rap scene.[[1]](#cite_note-Vice-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Slang-2)[[3]](#cite_note-IndieRocks-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Vice2-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Chidas-5) Influenced by North American hip-hop but rooted in Spanish rap, Hispana's lyrics reflect and amplify everyday life and are imbued with a social and feminist conscience.[[6]](#cite_note-RollingStone2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-poua-7)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mexican musician

| Hispana | |
| --- | --- |
| Also known as | * Mamba Negra * Mujer de fuego |
| Born | Patricia Polet González   [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), Mexico |
| Genres | * [Latin pop](/wiki/Latin_pop "Latin pop") * [hip hop](/wiki/Hip_hop_music "Hip hop music") |
| Occupations | * Rapper * singer * songwriter |
| Years active | 2012 (2012)–present |
| Label | Universal Music Mexico |

Musical artist

**Hispana**, also known as **Mamba Negra** is a Mexican rapper, singer and songwriter from [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), and is one of the most prominent female artists in the Mexican rap scene.[[1]](#cite_note-Vice-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Slang-2)[[3]](#cite_note-IndieRocks-3)[[4]](#cite_note-Vice2-4)[[5]](#cite_note-Chidas-5) Influenced by North American hip-hop but rooted in Spanish rap, Hispana's lyrics reflect and amplify everyday life and are imbued with a social and feminist conscience.[[6]](#cite_note-RollingStone2-6)[[7]](#cite_note-poua-7)

While she began rapping at the age of 13 in [Puerto Vallarta](/wiki/Puerto_Vallarta "Puerto Vallarta"), the release of her 7-track EP debut *Mamba Negra* in 2012 placed her on the Mexican underground hip hop radar.[[8]](#cite_note-RollingStone1-8)[[6]](#cite_note-RollingStone2-6)[[1]](#cite_note-Vice-1) Because she was widely recognized by her mixtape, Hispana decided to also take on the name Mamba Negra to identify herself.[[9]](#cite_note-Slang2-9) Her sophomore album, *Los Gonzalez* references her last name and is dedicated to her two younger brothers.[[1]](#cite_note-Vice-1)

Hispana signed with [Universal Music Mexico](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") and released her studio album, *Mujer de Fuego* in 2021. It features collaborations with [Niña Dioz](/w/index.php?title=Ni%C3%B1a_Dioz&action=edit&redlink=1 "Niña Dioz (page does not exist)"), and touches on themes of female empowerment, success and confidence, as well as her Mexican roots. The album was composed during Hispana's pregnancy and recorded during the first months of her maternity, which coincided with the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"). This resulted in a more an introspective album, but also forced Hispana to forego the traditional shows and extended promotion expected with a major release.[[3]](#cite_note-IndieRocks-3)[[8]](#cite_note-RollingStone1-8)[[7]](#cite_note-poua-7) Hispana's style has evolved to experiment with new genres and collaborations. In December 2021, she released the single *Andamos a cien* featuring Mexican rapper [Yoss Bones](/w/index.php?title=Yoss_Bones&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yoss Bones (page does not exist)"), with whom she has previously collaborated for the single *Tequila y Miel*.[[10]](#cite_note-Diario-10)

Hispana's confidence as a woman in the [Latin American](/wiki/Latin_America "Latin America") hip-hop scene has inspired many other female artists in their work,[[2]](#cite_note-Slang-2)[[10]](#cite_note-Diario-10) with her citing [Mercedes Sosa](/wiki/Mercedes_Sosa "Mercedes Sosa"), [Liliana Felipe](/w/index.php?title=Liliana_Felipe&action=edit&redlink=1 "Liliana Felipe (page does not exist)") and [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé").[[11]](#cite_note-Polvora-11) as own her personal musical inspirations.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hispana_(rapper)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Discography")]

* *Mamba Negra* (2012)
* *Los González* (2019)
* *Mudando de piel* (2020)
* *Mujer de fuego* (2021)
